title: Error: exec: "qemu-system-x86_64": executable file not found in $PATH after installing via homebrew on MacOS Monterey
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Installation with homebrew doesn't install/link qemu on macos.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install homebrew

2. run `brew install podman`

3. run `podman machine init`

**Describe the results you received:**

```bash
Error: exec: "qemu-system-x86_64": executable file not found in $PATH
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```bash
Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-35.20220103.2.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The fix was running `brew link qemu && brew reinstall qemu` but it would be ideal if this wasn't necessary.

Error when trying to start the machine after running `brew link qemu` and before `brew reinstall qemu`

```
j@MacBook-Pro ~ % podman machine start
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...
INFO[0000] listening tcp://127.0.0.1:7777
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/b5/yrr83mns5fs6sz4vykhjxgfm0000gn/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
Waiting for VM ...
qemu-system-x86_64: Unknown Error
Error: dial unix /var/folders/b5/yrr83mns5fs6sz4vykhjxgfm0000gn/T/podman/podman-machine-default_ready.sock: connect: connection refused
ERRO[0003] cannot receive packets from , disconnecting: cannot read size from socket: EOF
ERRO[0003] cannot read size from socket: EOF
```

I'm not yet sure if this is reproducible, if not I will close this but hoping this would provide some help if someone runs into the same issue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
j@MacBook-Pro ~ % podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec  8 12:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 15:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I have an issue almost identical to this on M1 Max:
```
➜  ~ podman machine start
INFO[0000] waiting for clients...
INFO[0000] listening tcp://127.0.0.1:7777
INFO[0000] new connection from  to /var/folders/_r/ym4h63j94l7b305575s09q200000gq/T/podman/qemu_podman-machine-default.sock
Error: fork/exec /usr/local/bin/qemu-system-x86_64: no such file or directory
ERRO[0000] cannot receive packets from , disconnecting: cannot read size from socket: EOF
ERRO[0000] cannot read size from socket: EOF
```

Let me update my mac and see if I can reproduce it, it might be a weird path error. 

@michaelkrasa, yours seems like a different issue, do you happen to have rosetta installed?
@michaelkrasa i would guess you do have rosetta installed which is why you are seeing calls to the intel qemu system.
my understanding is that if you ever used rosetta it changes the architecture in brew ... could this be it?

I was running into the same problem for few hours today @michaelkrasa - my first time trying to install/use podman.

I fixed it uninstalling podman, uninstalling brew, etc, and then installing all over again.

I'm assuming I had an old version of brew which I installed under emulation "arch -x86_64 zsh" January last year.

After that, I got the correct version of qemu when running podman machine init and podman machine start.

I'll give it one more try, already reinstalled the whole lot. I migrated
from an Intel MacBook and I can't think of anything it could be. I don't
have Rosetta installed btw.

On Thu, Jan 27, 2022, 09:12 Marco Antonio Blos de Souza <
***@***.***> wrote:

> init and podman machine start.

@michaelkrasa I have rosetta installed here, not sure that is needed or not, as I mentioned, first time using/installing podman. One important thing I found is that you have to install homebrew in a specific path when using Apple M1. It must be cloned and installed from /opt/homebrew.

I've finally fixed this issue, I reinstalled homebrew but that most likely wasn't the problem. I never removed the initial container that I was trying to start. Once I realized this mistake I just deleted it using `podman machine rm podman-machine-default` and ran `podman machine start` to finish the usual installation sequence.
I am assuming this is not an issue that Podman can fix, so closing.  Reopen if I am mistaken.

@michaelkrasa It seems, reinstalling homebrew DID the trick. This is a very strong measure (and I admit, I was sceptical ;), but I tried to delete the initial machine and that did not change anything. I don't know what caused the issue but completely reinstalling homebrew solved it...
@millawell I tried reinstalling Homebrew and it did not solve the issue that was first reported. I am using qemu 6.2 and podman 4.03
@pvgb if you do a `which qemu-system-x86_64` does it return anything?

@baude 
I'm facing the same issue but on Linux Ubuntu, I ran the commend you suggested but it was unable to locate the package.

❯ which qemu-system-x86_64
qemu-system-x86_64 not found

Had the same error on Ubuntu but here, but ` sudo apt install qemu-system-x86` did the job.

maybe a  `brew install qemu` works for you?

Here is the final fix for everyone... coming to this page later on...

```
brew install qemu
brew link qemu
```

Linux Mint (Ubuntu based) 

This worked for me:

```
sudo apt-get install qemu-system-x86

```
